judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully and correctly answer all four expectations; Report 1 is marginally more thorough, but they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: The PR should target **`main`**. ... The only other conceivable base, `feat/weighted-scorer` (the branch this work was stacked on), must not be used

report_2.evidence: **1. Base branch: `main`.** ... The only alternative would be stacking the PR on `feat/weighted-scorer`, the branch this work depends on — but that dependency has already landed on `main`

report_1.evidence: **`feat/pairwise-metric` was cut from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer`** ... landed as a **squash merge via PR #42** ... `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` returns false — by ancestry, `feat/weighted-scorer` was never merged. But by **content** it is fully merged

report_2.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was created from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` ... **it was squash-merged into `main`** ... the three W commits themselves are not ancestors of `main` and never will be

report_1.evidence: So no, not all of it is pairwise work. ... The other +16/−3 are the weighted-scorer changes, which are present for a purely mechanical reason: the branch's merge base with `main` predates the squash merge, so git's diff-from-merge-base re-shows the stacked foundation even though `main` already contains identical content via commit 1971f08 (#42)

report_2.evidence: **Not all of this is pairwise-metric work.** ... the entire scorer.py rewrite — is the `feat/weighted-scorer` work. It appears in the PR diff because the branch physically carries commits W1–W3, and the merge base (`65204ad`) predates the squash merge

report_1.evidence: **Rebase only the pairwise commits onto main**: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... **Verify the result**: `git merge-base --is-ancestor main HEAD` ... `git diff main...HEAD` now equals the 5-line net-new diff ... `git log main..HEAD -- <file>` is non-empty only for `scoring/metrics.py` and `prompts/grader.txt`

report_2.evidence: **Rebase only the pairwise commits onto main**: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... **Verify the result**: `git log --oneline main..feat/pairwise-metric` should show exactly 2 commits, and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` should now equal the 5-line pairwise-only delta
